seoTitle: How to Categorize Credit Card Rewards in QuickBooks
metaDescription: Learn how to categorize credit card rewards and cash back in QuickBooks so points and statement credits are tracked accurately in your books.
intro: Cash back and rewards points from a business credit card need a home in your books, even though they aren't typical income or expenses. This guide explains how to categorize credit card rewards in QuickBooks.

steps.heading: Decide how rewards should be categorized
steps.body: Determine whether the reward will be treated as other income or as a reduction to the related expense, based on your accountant's preference.

steps.heading: Set up a rewards account if needed
steps.body: Create a dedicated account for tracking credit card rewards if you want them visible as their own line item.

steps.heading: Record the reward transaction
steps.body: Enter the reward as a deposit or credit, assigning it to the account you've set up.

steps.heading: Reconcile the entry
steps.body: Match the recorded reward to the statement credit or deposit shown on your credit card statement.

faq.question: Are credit card rewards taxable income?
faq.answer: This can depend on how the rewards were earned and your business structure, so it's worth checking with an accountant.

faq.question: Should rewards reduce an expense or count as income?
faq.answer: Both approaches are used in practice; the right choice often depends on your bookkeeping preferences and accountant's guidance.

faq.question: How do I record rewards redeemed for a statement credit?
faq.answer: A statement credit can be recorded as a credit against your credit card account balance.

faq.question: Can I track rewards separately from regular transactions?
faq.answer: Yes, setting up a dedicated account makes it easy to see total rewards earned over time.
